text:
Below is the detailed logic analysis for the dataset_loader.py module. This analysis explains stepвЂђbyвЂђstep how the DatasetLoader class should work, what computations it must perform, and how it packages the results into a GraphData structure.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
1. OVERALL OBJECTIVE

вЂў The DatasetLoader will load a graph dataset (e.g., Pubmed as specified in config.yaml), compute its key components, and package these into a GraphData object.
вЂў The key items to compute include:
вЂѓвЂ“ The adjacency matrix (A) from the graph.
вЂѓвЂ“ Node features (X) and labels (Y) for the node classification task.
вЂѓвЂ“ The normalized Laplacian (L) computed by L = I вЂ“ D^(вЂ“ВЅ) A D^(вЂ“ВЅ).
вЂѓвЂ“ A (truncated) eigen-decomposition of the Laplacian that yields a set of eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
вЂў These results are then stored in GraphData as attributes used later by the synthetic graph distillation module.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
2. LOADING THE DATA

вЂў Based on the configuration (config.yaml: dataset.name = "Pubmed" and split_method = "public"), the loader must locate the Pubmed dataset.
вЂѓвЂ“ This will typically involve reading from files or an online source.
вЂѓвЂ“ The loader should extract:
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ The node feature matrix X (as a NumPy array of size [N, d]).
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ The edge list to build the graph.
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ The labels Y for each node.
вЂў Use NetworkX to build a graph from the edge list and any associated node information.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
3. COMPUTING THE ADJACENCY MATRIX

вЂў Once the NetworkX graph is built, convert it to an adjacency matrix.
вЂѓвЂ“ Use NetworkX functions (such as nx.adjacency_matrix) to obtain A as either a sparse or dense NumPy array.
вЂѓвЂ“ Since the graph is undirected (and assumed connected), A will be symmetric.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
4. COMPUTING THE NORMALIZED LAPLACIAN

вЂў Calculate the degree for each node: for node i, degree d_i = sum(A[i, :]).
вЂѓвЂ“ Ensure that all nodes have nonzero degree (the connected graph assumption should hold).
вЂў Compute the inverse square root of the degree for each node.
вЂў Form the normalized Laplacian using the formula:
вЂѓвЂѓL = I вЂ“ D^(вЂ“ВЅ) A D^(вЂ“ВЅ)
вЂѓвЂ“ Here, I is the identity matrix.
вЂў Use NumPy for matrix operations (or use sparse operations if A is stored sparsely).

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
5. SETTING UP FOR TRUNCATED EIGEN-DECOMPOSITION

вЂў The paper only requires a truncated number of eigenpairs for matching the eigenbasis:
вЂѓвЂ“ According to the configuration вЂњsynth_graph.compression_ratioвЂќ (0.15) and вЂњsynth_graph.r_kвЂќ (0.9), compute the synthetic graph size NвЂІ.
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ Determine NвЂІ = int(N * (compression_ratio / 100)) if the value is given as a percentageвЂ”in our case, the comment вЂњ0.15%вЂќ indicates that compression_ratio should be interpreted as a percent.
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ For example, if N is 19,717 (as in Pubmed), then NвЂІ в‰€ int(19717 * 0.0015) в‰€ 30.
вЂѓвЂ“ Since the paper requires matching K = Kв‚Ѓ + Kв‚‚ eigenvectors (with K = NвЂІ), let:
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ Kв‚Ѓ = int(NвЂІ * r_k)вЂѓвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓ (e.g., 90% of 30 в‰€ 27)
вЂѓвЂѓвЂ“ Kв‚‚ = NвЂІ вЂ“ Kв‚ЃвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓвЂѓ (e.g., remainder, в‰€ 3)
вЂў These computed values (Kв‚Ѓ and Kв‚‚) define how many eigenpairs to extract from the Laplacian.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
6. PERFORMING TRUNCATED EIGEN-DECOMPOSITION

вЂў Use the SciPy sparse linear algebra routines (scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh) to compute the eigenpairs of L:
вЂѓвЂ“ For the Kв‚Ѓ smallest eigenpairs, call eigsh with parameter k = Kв‚Ѓ and which='SM' (smallest magnitude).
вЂѓвЂ“ For the Kв‚‚ largest eigenpairs, call eigsh with parameter k = Kв‚‚ and which='LA' (largest algebraic).
вЂў Concatenate the eigenvalues and eigenvectors appropriately:
вЂѓвЂ“ Order them as the Kв‚Ѓ smallest first and then the Kв‚‚ largest.
вЂѓвЂ“ The resulting eigenvectors matrix should have shape [N, (Kв‚Ѓ+Kв‚‚)].
вЂў Note any caveats:
вЂѓвЂ“ Ensure that L is symmetric and real.
вЂѓвЂ“ If the matrix is large, using the sparse eigendecomposition is essential for efficiency.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
7. PACKAGING INTO GRAPHDATA STRUCTURE

вЂў Create a GraphData class instance (a simple container) that has the following attributes:
вЂѓвЂ“ adjacency: the computed adjacency matrix (np.ndarray)
вЂѓвЂ“ node_features: X (np.ndarray)
вЂѓвЂ“ labels: Y (np.ndarray)
вЂѓвЂ“ laplacian: L (np.ndarray)
вЂѓвЂ“ eigenvalues: the concatenated eigenvalues from the two groups (np.ndarray with shape [Kв‚Ѓ+Kв‚‚])
вЂѓвЂ“ eigenvectors: the concatenated eigenvectors (np.ndarray with shape [N, Kв‚Ѓ+Kв‚‚])
вЂў Also include any additional relevant data such as the synthetic graph size NвЂІ. This may be used later in the model initialization.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
8. ERROR HANDLING & LOGGING

вЂў Include error checking for:
вЂѓвЂ“ File/data not found errors when loading the dataset.
вЂѓвЂ“ Inconsistencies in node degrees (if any node has zero degree).
вЂѓвЂ“ Potential issues during eigen-decomposition.
вЂў Log key shapes and values (e.g., number of nodes N, shape of adjacency, laplacian, and the truncated eigenvector matrix) for debugging purposes.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
9. DEPENDENCIES

вЂў This module will import:
вЂѓвЂ“ numpy for general array operations.
вЂѓвЂ“ scipy (particularly scipy.sparse.linalg.eigsh) for eigen-decomposition.
вЂѓвЂ“ networkx for graph loading and conversion into an adjacency matrix.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
10. SUMMARY OF THE DATAFLOW

a) The DatasetLoader is initialized with a configuration dictionary.
вЂѓвЂ“ It reads the dataset name and parameters (e.g., compression_ratio) from config.
b) It loads the graph (in our case, вЂњPubmedвЂќ) and extracts node features, labels, and constructs the networkx graph.
c) The adjacency matrix A is computed, and from it, the normalized Laplacian L is derived.
d) Based on the compression_ratio and r_k from config, it calculates the number of eigenpairs to extract (Kв‚Ѓ smallest and Kв‚‚ largest).
e) It performs the truncated eigen-decomposition using eigsh.
f) All computed components (A, X, Y, L, eigenvalues, eigenvectors) are stored in an instance of GraphData.
g) Finally, the GraphData object is returned to be used by the training and evaluation modules.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
This logic analysis outlines the stepвЂђbyвЂђstep processing in dataset_loader.py and ensures that each key component of the graph data is correctly computed and made available in a structured way for downstream synthetic graph distillation tasks.
